zone_6b_notes: Start indoors 6â€“8 weeks before last frost (February in Farmington) for spring crop, or direct-sow in late July for fall crop. Spring broccoli is a race against Missouri heat â€” transplant out as early as late March and harvest before June. Fall broccoli is more reliable and flavorful; frost sweetens the heads. Harvest heads while tight and dark green â€” yellow florets mean it has gone past. Side shoots continue producing for weeks after main head harvest. Cabbage loopers and imported cabbageworms are the main pest threat in Missouri.

planting.best_planting_time: Transplant late March to early April (spring); direct sow late July (fall)
